name: United Kingdom (Blue Ensign)

colors.symbolism: the field, from the Royal Navy's historic Blue Squadron
the red details of the Union canton
the white details of the Union canton

description: A dark blue field with the Union Flag in the canton; flown plain by government vessels or defaced with a departmental or territorial badge.
history: Originally the ensign of the Royal Navy's Blue Squadron, the Blue Ensign became the ensign of British government (non-military) vessels in 1864. Defaced with badges it serves the Royal Naval Reserve, government departments, and many British territories and Commonwealth states as the basis of their own ensigns.

features.symbolism: the historic colour of the Royal Navy's Blue Squadron and of British government service
the Union Flag, marking the ensign as British
